sources.title: Pharmacokinetic study of 11-Keto beta-Boswellic acid
A double blind, randomized, placebo controlled study of the efficacy and safety of 5-Loxin for treatment of osteoarthritis of the knee
Double-blind placebo-controlled pilot trial of acemannan in advanced human immunodeficiency virus disease
Alpha-, beta-, and gamma-asarone: Current status of toxicological evaluation
General toxicity studies of alpha mangostin from Garcinia mangostana: A systematic review
The pharmacology activities of Angelica keiskei Koidzumi and its efficacy and safety in humans
Atractylenolides (I, II, and III): a review of their pharmacology and pharmacokinetics
A review of the pharmacology and toxicology of aucubin
Food, Acid Supplementation and Drug Absorption - a Complicated Gastric Mix: a Randomized Control Trial
Oral herbal therapies for treating osteoarthritis
Clinical toxicology study of an herbal medicinal extract of Paullinia cupana, Trichilia catigua, Ptychopetalum olacoides and Zingiber officinale in healthy volunteers
A randomized controlled clinical trial examining the effects of Cordyceps militaris beverage on the immune response in healthy adults
Efficacy and Safety of Cordyceps militaris as an Adjuvant to Duloxetine in the Treatment of Insomnia in Patients With Depression
Diosgenin-Rich Yam Extract Enhances Cognitive Function: A Placebo-Controlled, Randomized, Double-Blind, Crossover Study of Healthy Adults
Safety of water extract of Cistanche tubulosa stems as a Novel food pursuant to Regulation (EU) 2015/2283
Unveiling the role of erinacines in the neuroprotective effects of Hericium erinaceus: a systematic review in preclinical models
Benefits, side effects, and uses of Hericium erinaceus as a supplement: a systematic review
Garcinol as an Epigenetic Modulator: Mechanisms of Anti-Cancer Activity and Therapeutic Potential
Guggulipid for the treatment of hypercholesterolemia: a randomized controlled trial
Wax-matrix extended-release niacin vs inositol hexanicotinate
Exploring the chemical and pharmacological variability of Lepidium meyenii: a comprehensive review of the effects of maca
The Efficacy and Safety of 1% Oxyresveratrol for Treating Facial Melasma on Sensitive Skin in Women
Efficacy and tolerability of an undenatured type II collagen supplement in modulating knee osteoarthritis symptoms
Safety of betaine as a novel food pursuant to Regulation (EC) No 258/97
The benefits and risks of beetroot juice consumption: a systematic review

changes.rationale: Human evidence is limited to Boswellia extract exposure and does not establish a constituent-specific pregnancy contraindication.

changes.expected_old_value: Boswellia extracts can cause GI upset and may interact with anticoagulants/anti-inflammatory drugs; pregnancy safety is not established.
changes.new_value: A single-dose pharmacokinetic study of Boswellia serrata extract in 12 healthy men reported no adverse effects, but it did not evaluate isolated 11-keto-beta-boswellic acid. Pregnancy, breastfeeding, repeated-dose, surgery, bleeding, liver, kidney, and medication-interaction safety remain insufficient.
changes.rationale: Separates whole-extract short-term findings from isolated-constituent safety.
The trial did not establish pregnancy, liver, or kidney contraindications.

changes.expected_old_value: Mostly preclinical or source-extract evidence. Isolated-compound safety is not equivalent to food/herb exposure; use caution with pregnancy, liver/kidney disease, and polypharmacy.
changes.new_value: In one 48-week trial in 63 men with advanced HIV disease, oral acemannan had no significant adverse-event difference from placebo and no significant pharmacokinetic interaction with zidovudine. This narrow study does not establish safety in pregnancy, breastfeeding, liver or kidney impairment, other populations, or with other medications.
changes.rationale: Adds the available human result while preserving its population and interaction limits.
AKBA-enriched extract trials do not establish a constituent-specific pregnancy contraindication.

changes.expected_old_value: Boswellia extracts can cause GI upset and may interact with anticoagulants/anti-inflammatory drugs; pregnancy safety is not established.
changes.new_value: A 90-day trial evaluated a specific Boswellia extract enriched to 30% AKBA with laboratory safety monitoring; that product-level evidence cannot establish the safety of isolated AKBA. Pregnancy, breastfeeding, surgery, bleeding, liver, kidney, and medication-interaction safety remain insufficient.
changes.rationale: Prevents extrapolation from a standardized extract to an isolated constituent.
Human safety is unknown and preclinical reproductive findings cannot be converted into a definitive human contraindication.

changes.expected_old_value: High-caution aromatic compound with toxicology concerns; avoid concentrated internal use and pregnancy.
changes.new_value: Human efficacy and safety are unknown. A toxicology review identified preclinical cardiac, hepatic, reproductive, mutagenic, and carcinogenic signals for propenylic asarone isomers, with varying reliability; these findings warrant concern but do not define human dose, incidence, or disease-specific contraindications.
changes.rationale: Labels the toxicology as preclinical and removes unsupported human guidance.
The systematic toxicity review was based on animal studies and did not establish a human liver contraindication.

changes.expected_old_value: Mostly preclinical/extract-derived; concentrated Garcinia/mangosteen extracts may affect bleeding or liver risk depending product.
changes.new_value: The systematic toxicity evidence for alpha-mangostin is preclinical and cannot establish oral human safety thresholds. Human pregnancy, breastfeeding, bleeding, surgery, liver, kidney, and medication-interaction safety remain insufficient; whole-mangosteen findings should not be assigned to isolated alpha-mangostin.
changes.rationale: Removes unsupported bleeding and liver claims and states the human evidence boundary.

changes.expected_old_value: Human safety data are limited; caution pregnancy, anticoagulants, and liver disease until better data exist.
changes.new_value: Small human studies summarized in a review reported mild or no adverse events, but preparations, doses, populations, and reporting varied. Pregnancy, breastfeeding, surgery, long-term use, liver or kidney impairment, and clinically significant medication interactions remain insufficiently characterized.
changes.rationale: Retains the limited human tolerability signal without inventing specific interactions.
The review does not support isolated-compound pregnancy, liver, or kidney contraindications.

changes.expected_old_value: Mostly preclinical or source-extract evidence. Isolated-compound safety is not equivalent to food/herb exposure; use caution with pregnancy, liver/kidney disease, and polypharmacy.
changes.new_value: Evidence for isolated atractylenolide I is primarily in vitro and animal-based. A review noted possible metabolic-enzyme inhibition as a research concern, but human dose-response, pregnancy, breastfeeding, liver, kidney, surgery, and medication-interaction safety have not been established.
changes.rationale: States the review's evidence level without presenting preclinical enzyme findings as a clinical interaction.
The review does not support isolated-compound pregnancy, liver, or kidney contraindications.

changes.expected_old_value: Mostly preclinical or source-extract evidence. Isolated-compound safety is not equivalent to food/herb exposure; use caution with pregnancy, liver/kidney disease, and polypharmacy.
changes.new_value: Evidence for isolated atractylenolide II is primarily in vitro and animal-based and is sparser than for related atractylenolides. Human dose-response, pregnancy, breastfeeding, liver, kidney, surgery, and medication-interaction safety have not been established.
changes.rationale: Replaces generic cautions with the documented evidence limitation.
The review does not support isolated-compound pregnancy, liver, or kidney contraindications.

changes.expected_old_value: Mostly preclinical or source-extract evidence. Isolated-compound safety is not equivalent to food/herb exposure; use caution with pregnancy, liver/kidney disease, and polypharmacy.
changes.new_value: Evidence for isolated atractylenolide III is primarily in vitro and animal-based. A review noted possible metabolic-enzyme inhibition as a research concern, but human dose-response, pregnancy, breastfeeding, liver, kidney, surgery, and medication-interaction safety have not been established.
changes.rationale: States the review's evidence level without converting preclinical findings into clinical guidance.
No human disease-specific contraindication was established; the field remains intentionally blank.

changes.expected_old_value: Mostly extract-derived/preclinical. GI effects and herb-specific contraindications matter more than isolated-compound evidence.
changes.new_value: A pharmacology and toxicology review found the aucubin evidence largely preclinical and called for clinical studies. Human dose-response, adverse-event incidence, pregnancy, breastfeeding, surgery, liver, kidney, and medication-interaction safety remain insufficient.
changes.rationale: Removes an unsupported GI statement and documents the lack of clinical safety evidence.
Small gastric-pH studies did not establish categorical GI-disease contraindications.

changes.expected_old_value: Avoid with ulcers, GERD, gastritis, or NSAID-related gastric injury risk.
changes.new_value: In a nine-person study, single betaine HCl doses up to 4,500 mg rapidly lowered post-meal gastric pH. Because gastric pH can change absorption of pH-sensitive medicines, medication-specific review is needed; repeated-use, ulcer, reflux, gastritis, pregnancy, breastfeeding, liver, and kidney safety were not established by this study.
changes.rationale: Uses observed gastric acidification and avoids unsupported disease contraindications.
Component-level assessments do not establish a product-specific human contraindication for betaine nitrate.

changes.expected_old_value: Component-based cautions: GI upset, LDL effects, nitrate vasodilation.
changes.new_value: Product-specific human safety evidence for betaine nitrate is insufficient. Safety findings for betaine, dietary nitrate, beetroot, or other nitrate salts cannot be combined to infer this compound's dose-response, blood-pressure effect, interactions, pregnancy, breastfeeding, surgery, liver, or kidney safety.
changes.rationale: Removes component-based extrapolation and records the formulation-specific evidence gap.
Trials and systematic review did not establish a categorical contraindication.

changes.expected_old_value: Use caution with anticoagulants and pregnancy; GI side effects can occur.
changes.new_value: Short osteoarthritis trials of specific Boswellia extracts, including an AKBA-enriched product, did not show a clear excess of adverse events, but reporting and formulations varied. Pregnancy, breastfeeding, surgery, bleeding, liver, kidney, long-term use, and medication-specific interaction safety remain insufficient.
changes.rationale: Preserves short-term tolerability while removing unsupported anticoagulant guidance.
The available human study used a multi-herb product and cannot establish a catuaba-specific contraindication.

changes.new_value: A 28-day healthy-volunteer study reported no severe adverse reactions or laboratory changes for a multi-herb product containing Trichilia catigua, but the result cannot be attributed to catuaba alone. Standalone-product, species-specific, pregnancy, breastfeeding, surgery, liver, kidney, and interaction safety remain insufficient.
changes.rationale: Adds the human finding while preventing multi-ingredient extrapolation.
Short trials did not establish a categorical contraindication.

changes.expected_old_value: Well tolerated with minimal adverse effects
changes.new_value: An eight-week fermented-beverage trial in healthy adults found no between-group differences in blood sugar, lipids, liver, kidney, or blood safety indices; a six-week adjunctive trial with duloxetine found no adverse-event incidence difference from placebo. These small, product-specific studies do not establish long-term, pregnancy, breastfeeding, autoimmune, transplant, or broader interaction safety.
changes.rationale: Replaces a blanket tolerability claim with two bounded human results.
A diosgenin-rich yam extract trial did not establish an isolated-diosgenin pregnancy contraindication.

changes.expected_old_value: Mostly preclinical or food/herb-associated. Caution with hormone-sensitive conditions, pregnancy, and anticoagulant/antidiabetic medication contexts.
changes.new_value: A 12-week trial of a diosgenin-rich yam extract in healthy adults reported no adverse effects, but that formulation is not equivalent to isolated diosgenin. Isolated-compound endocrine, pregnancy, breastfeeding, bleeding, blood-sugar, liver, kidney, and medication-interaction safety remain insufficient.
changes.rationale: Removes inferred hormonal and medication cautions while retaining the product-level trial result.
The EFSA assessment did not establish an isolated-echinacoside contraindication.

changes.expected_old_value: Mostly preclinical/extract-derived; safety depends on source herb and product quality.
changes.new_value: EFSA did not establish the safety of a Cistanche tubulosa extract containing 25-45% echinacoside after human adverse-event and toxicology concerns. Those events cannot be attributed to echinacoside alone, so isolated-compound dose-response, pregnancy, breastfeeding, bleeding, seizure, liver, kidney, and interaction safety remain unresolved.
changes.rationale: Captures the regulatory concern without assigning extract events to one constituent.
Human whole-mushroom studies and preclinical erinacine studies do not establish isolated-erinacine contraindications.

changes.expected_old_value: Mostly preclinical or source-extract evidence. Isolated-compound safety is not equivalent to food/herb exposure; use caution with pregnancy, liver/kidney disease, and polypharmacy.
changes.new_value: A systematic review of erinacines evaluated cellular and animal models, while human safety reports concern whole Hericium erinaceus preparations. Whole-mushroom stomach discomfort, headache, or allergy reports cannot define isolated-erinacine risk; pregnancy, breastfeeding, liver, kidney, surgery, and interaction safety remain insufficient.
changes.rationale: Separates constituent evidence from whole-mushroom adverse-event reporting.
The review reports that human trials are lacking and does not establish a liver contraindication.

changes.expected_old_value: Mostly preclinical/extract-derived; concentrated Garcinia/mangosteen extracts may affect bleeding or liver risk depending product.
changes.new_value: Garcinol evidence remains preclinical, and a recent review found human trials lacking. Human dose-response, pregnancy, breastfeeding, bleeding, surgery, liver, kidney, chemotherapy, and medication-interaction safety cannot be inferred from Garcinia extracts or cell and animal studies.
changes.rationale: Removes unsupported bleeding and liver claims and states the translational limit.
A standardized guggul extract trial does not establish an isolated-guggulsterone pregnancy contraindication.

changes.expected_old_value: May cause GI irritation or hemolysis risk in isolated/high-dose contexts; herb-specific safety and pregnancy cautions apply.
changes.new_value: In an eight-week trial, standardized guggul extract containing 2.5% guggulsterones caused hypersensitivity rash in six treated participants versus none receiving placebo. This cannot determine isolated-guggulsterone incidence or establish pregnancy, breastfeeding, liver, kidney, endocrine, surgery, or medication-interaction safety.
changes.rationale: Replaces unsupported hemolysis and pregnancy claims with the documented product-level adverse event.
The trial did not establish a liver contraindication for inositol hexanicotinate.

changes.expected_old_value: Less flushing than nicotinic acid; rare hepatotoxicity possible; may mislead if used as lipid-lowering substitute.
changes.new_value: In a six-week trial, 1,500 mg/day inositol hexanicotinate was well tolerated but showed no lipid benefit over placebo and no measurable bioavailability in a small pharmacokinetic subset. Long-term, pregnancy, breastfeeding, liver-disease, kidney-disease, and medication-interaction safety remain insufficient; safety claims for other niacin forms should not be transferred to this form.
changes.rationale: Removes an unsupported hepatotoxicity claim and uses formulation-specific human evidence.
The review did not establish a categorical contraindication for maca extract.

changes.new_value: Human studies summarized in a review generally reported good short-term tolerance with rare adverse effects, but maca chemistry varies by ecotype, processing, and formulation. Pregnancy, breastfeeding, hormone-sensitive conditions, liver or kidney impairment, long-term use, and medication-interaction safety remain insufficient.
changes.rationale: Qualifies tolerability by the review's product-variability limits.
Whole-maca evidence does not establish contraindications for isolated macamides.

changes.expected_old_value: Limited safety data as isolated compounds
changes.new_value: Human tolerability evidence concerns whole maca preparations, whose macamide content varies with ecotype and processing. Isolated-macamide dose-response, adverse-event incidence, pregnancy, breastfeeding, endocrine, liver, kidney, surgery, and medication-interaction safety remain insufficient.
changes.rationale: Defines why whole-maca safety cannot be assigned to isolated macamides.
Topical evidence does not support an oral categorical contraindication.

changes.expected_old_value: May affect bleeding risk and drug metabolism at supplement doses; GI upset and headache are possible.
changes.new_value: Available human trial evidence identified here is topical and cannot establish oral supplement safety. Oral dose-response, bleeding, blood pressure, blood sugar, pregnancy, breastfeeding, surgery, liver, kidney, and medication-interaction safety should not be inferred from resveratrol or laboratory metabolism studies.
changes.rationale: Removes unsupported oral adverse effects and prevents resveratrol-to-oxyresveratrol extrapolation.
The trial did not establish a categorical contraindication.

changes.expected_old_value: Generally well tolerated in trials; avoid if allergic to source material.
changes.new_value: A 180-day osteoarthritis trial of a specific undenatured type II collagen product found no safety-outcome difference from placebo or glucosamine-chondroitin. Product-source allergy, pregnancy, breastfeeding, autoimmune disease, long-term use, liver, kidney, and medication-interaction safety were not adequately established.
changes.rationale: Removes an unsourced allergy directive while retaining bounded trial tolerability.
